symptoms.symptom_name: Explosive widespread urticaria with flushing and positive dermatographism

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid widespread urticaria/flushing with dermatographism after heat is the cutaneous MCAS phenotype.
Urticaria alone (without airway/CVS/GI features) is not anaphylaxis.
Absence of respiratory/airway involvement argues strongly against current anaphylaxis, though progression must be safety-netted.
No circulatory compromise argues against anaphylactic shock.
Absence of GI involvement further lowers the likelihood of systemic anaphylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Failure of a single standard-dose OTC antihistamine

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe flares often need up-dosed non-sedating H1 antihistamines rather than a single standard dose.
Mild tachycardia fits distress/histamine; marked tachycardia with hypotension would signal anaphylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patent airway, clear chest, normotensive, confluent urticaria/dermatographism (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A patent airway, clear chest and normal BP with confluent urticaria confirm a purely cutaneous event in person.
No airway/chest/CVS compromise on exam excludes current anaphylaxis.
Known MCAS with heat trigger supports a cutaneous degranulation flare — but also flags future anaphylaxis risk.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Explosive urticaria, flushing and dermatographism WITHOUT airway, circulatory or GI involvement is an isolated cutaneous mast cell flare — not anaphylaxis. It is managed with antihistamine up-dosing and cooling, with a very low threshold to escalate if any systemic feature appears; the patient's MCAS status also flags the need to check for an adrenaline autoinjector.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anaphylaxis symptom screen (breathing, throat/voice, circulation, GI) — repeat over time
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detects progression to anaphylaxis; the key remote safety step.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Serum tryptase (if seen acutely in person) and allergy/immunology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To support MCAS work-up and management; not a remote acute step.
Given MCAS and living alone, ensure access to and training in an autoinjector and an ASCIA action plan.
